title: Week 11 Lecture PDF: Cybersecurity and IoT
summary: Lecture PDF for Week 11 with cybersecurity, IoT, analytics, and emerging database topic material.

Page 6
Reflection and Peer Assessment
Last Individual Assessment
Worth 10%
-
That's because it's important
Reflection
-
Questions about your thoughts (What did you find interesting, challenging, what
would you do differently, ....)
Self-Assessment
-
How do you think you performed, as a team member and individual learner.
Assessments of Teammates
-
How did each of your teammates perform?
Template
-
Link from the Submission page for Reflection and Peer Assessment

Page 7
Reflection
Prompts to answer
The most important things I learnt:
-
SQL will come in handy. Grouping functions are useful for a simple analysis. I'll be
able to create a simple relational model for a new scenario.
The things that helped me most were:
-
My tutor.
I found the following topics particularly challenging:
-
Normalisation.
I found the following topics particularly interesting:
-
Nothing.
I feel I learnt these topics, concepts, and/or tools really well:
-
I'm good at using MySQL, I can make sense of its error messages. I can look them
up by code.
I still need to work on the following areas:
-
...
Great. Remember to fill in
the SFT. But how about:
"He/she showed me how to
find solutions on stack
overflow."

Page 8
Self- and Peer Assessment
Same Questions to Answer
One series of questions for you, one for each teammate
-
Contributing to the Team's Work
-
Interacting with Teammates
-
Keeping the Team on Track
-
Expecting Quality
-
Having related knowledge, skills and abilities
-
Did you participate in a process of performance improvement of a teammate?
-
Responses
-
High, medium or low… and how you arrived at this conclusion

Page 9
Self- and Peer Assessment
Example: Contributing to the Team's Work
High
-
Does more or higher-quality work than expected.
-
Makes important contributions that improve the team's work.
-
Helps teammates who are having difficulty completing their work.
Medium
-
Completes a fair share of the team's work with acceptable quality.
-
Keeps commitments and completes assignments on time.
-
Helps teammates who are having difficulty when it is easy or important.
Low
-
Does not do a fair share of the team's work. Delivers sloppy or incomplete work.

Page 10
Example Peer Assessment
Page Heading: Teammate Wilbur Nguyen
Contributing to the Team's work
-
High. Led the iterations of the diagram. Clarified the use cases and refined the
SQL queries. Chased up contributions from others.
etc.
Avoid this style:
-
High. Made important contributions that improved the team's work.

Page 11
Final Report and Product Deliverable
Process and Product Assessment
PDF of custom exported Confluence Space, containing
-
Initial ER diagram (Week 4)
-
Review of ER diagram to normalise/denormalise, revise relationships (Week 5),
possible subsequent reviews (e.g. adjustments due to use cases)
-
Physical database (Week 6) - Create Table statements
-
Document on data creation and null values (Week 7)
-
Use cases and SQL statements, transactions (Week 8)
-
Performance (indexes) (Week 9)
-
Major-specific work (naming the contributor) (Week 10 + 11)
-
Task Management
-
Team reflection (4L Retrospective) (Week 12)

Page 16
Process and Product Video
3-minute video
Content
-
Title slide with team names;
-
Introduction and project background, summary of requirements;
-
Mission statement and objectives;
-
1-2 slides about team process (collaboration, roles, contributions to solution);
-
1-3 slides about the logical/physical design, discussion of decisions made
regarding the entities and their relationships;
-
Discussion of solutions to use cases (e.g. SQL, also the design solutions
influenced by these use cases - there is overlap with item 5 here);
-
1-5 slides on major-specific technology;
-
Summary of solution and lessons learnt.
